applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to optimize reduction operations (min, sum). Used Triton @triton.jit kernel for fused GELU + bias operation. The Triton kernel is wrapped with torch.compiler.disable to avoid compatibility issues with torch.compile's TTIR conversion.

attempted_but_failed.name: Full Triton kernel for min+sum+GELU+bias
attempted_but_failed.reason: Nested loops over height (256) caused high overhead, slower than PyTorch

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile mode=max-autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: Caused severe performance degradation due to CUDAGraph issues

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton kernel without torch.compiler.disable
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues when torch.compile tries to convert Triton kernel to TTIR

attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent produced no output again in trial 8 — matching the pattern of 7 out of 8 trials with zero output due to infrastructure/container issues. Best achieved score is 62.60 vs target 66.0. With ~30 min

notes: Manual Triton kernels for full fusion were slower than PyTorch due to loop overhead
Best approach: torch.compile for reductions + Triton kernel for elementwise GELU+bias
Triton kernel must be wrapped with torch.compiler.disable to avoid TTIR conversion issues
